    async def test_tick_suspends_while_provider_setup_not_validated(
        self,
        hass: HomeAssistant,
        mock_lock_config_entry,
        lock_code_manager_config_entry,
    ) -> None:
        """A lock whose provider setup has not validated suspends sync quietly.

        A structurally-degraded lock (e.g. zero usable PIN slots after a
        re-inclusion) is kept in runtime data with entities, but attempting
        writes against it would fail on the capability probe and land in the
        generic error handler — raising a misleading "report this bug"
        suspension repair on top of the accurate lock_setup_failed one, and
        re-firing the recovery device query on every attempt. The tick must
        suspend without calling _perform_sync, and resume via
        request_sync_check once validation succeeds.
        """
        entity_obj = get_in_sync_entity_obj(hass, SLOT_1_IN_SYNC_ENTITY)
        manager = entity_obj._sync_manager

        manager._state = SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC
        manager._coordinator.data[1] = SlotCredential.empty()
        manager._lock._setup_succeeded = False

        with patch.object(
            manager, "_perform_sync", new_callable=AsyncMock
        ) as perform_sync:
            await manager._async_tick()
            await hass.async_block_till_done()

        perform_sync.assert_not_called()
        assert manager._state is SyncState.SUSPENDED

        # Validation succeeds later (re-interview + integration reload); the
        # next sync-check request resumes reconciliation.
        manager._lock._setup_succeeded = True
        manager.request_sync_check()
        assert manager._state is SyncState.OUT_OF_SYNC

    async def test_suspended_does_not_resume_while_setup_not_validated(
        self,
        hass: HomeAssistant,
        mock_lock_config_entry,
        lock_code_manager_config_entry,
    ) -> None:
        """request_sync_check keeps an unvalidated lock suspended even when reachable."""
        entity_obj = get_in_sync_entity_obj(hass, SLOT_1_IN_SYNC_ENTITY)
        manager = entity_obj._sync_manager

        manager._state = SyncState.SUSPENDED
        manager._code_suspend_target = None
        manager._lock._setup_succeeded = False
        assert manager._coordinator.unreachable is False

        manager.request_sync_check()
        assert manager._state is SyncState.SUSPENDED

def connection_check_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
        """
        Return interval for connection state checks.

        Defaults to 30 seconds. Returns None to disable periodic checks.
        """
        return timedelta(seconds=30)

    @property
    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
        """
        Return whether this lock supports push-based updates.

        When True, the lock will receive real-time value updates via
        subscribe_push_updates() instead of periodic polling. Polling is
        still used for initial load and drift detection (hard_refresh_interval).
        """
        return False

@property
    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
        """
        Return whether this lock supports push-based updates.

        When True, the lock will receive real-time value updates via
        subscribe_push_updates() instead of periodic polling. Polling is
        still used for initial load and drift detection (hard_refresh_interval).
        """
        return False

    @final
    @property
    def provider_setup_succeeded(self) -> bool:
        """
        Return whether provider setup (including capability validation) succeeded.

        False both while setup is deferred (provider integration still
        loading) and after a structural validation failure (e.g. the lock
        reports zero usable PIN slots). Sync managers gate write attempts
        on this so a degraded lock is not hammered with operations that
        fail on the capability probe; the LOADED-transition revalidation
        flips it back and reconciliation resumes.
        """
        return self._setup_succeeded

        flow_id, {CONF_LOCKS: [LOCK_1_ENTITY_ID, LOCK_2_ENTITY_ID]}

    assert result["type"] == "abort"
    assert result["reason"] == "locks_updated"


async def test_reauth_wins_over_stale_options(
    hass: HomeAssistant, mock_lock_config_entry, lock_code_manager_config_entry
):
    """A reauth fix is not reverted by stale options saved while the entry was failed.

    While an entry is failed (e.g. a configured lock entity vanished after a
    Z-Wave exclusion) no update listener is registered, so an options-flow
    save just sits in ``options``. The data→options migration merges
    options-preferred, so a reauth that only wrote into ``data`` would lose
    to the stale options on the next load. The reauth write must therefore
    consume the pending options (options-preferred, its own input winning)
    and clear them.
    """
    entry = lock_code_manager_config_entry
    entry.async_start_reauth(hass, context={"lock_entity_id": LOCK_1_ENTITY_ID})
    await hass.async_block_till_done()

    # An options-flow save the failed entry could never process.
    stale_options = {**BASE_CONFIG, CONF_LOCKS: [LOCK_2_ENTITY_ID]}
    hass.config_entries.async_update_entry(entry, options=stale_options)

    [flow] = entry.async_get_active_flows(hass, {SOURCE_REAUTH})
    result = await hass.config_entries.flow.async_configure(
        flow["flow_id"], {CONF_LOCKS: [LOCK_1_ENTITY_ID, LOCK_2_ENTITY_ID]}
    )
    assert result["reason"] == "locks_updated"
    await hass.async_block_till_done()

    # The reauth's lock list won and the stale options were consumed, so the
    # next data→options migration has nothing stale to prefer.
    assert entry.data[CONF_LOCKS] == [LOCK_1_ENTITY_ID, LOCK_2_ENTITY_ID]
    assert not entry.options

async def test_reauth_wins_over_stale_options(
    hass: HomeAssistant, mock_lock_config_entry, lock_code_manager_config_entry
):
    """A reauth fix is not reverted by stale options saved while the entry was failed.

    While an entry is failed (e.g. a configured lock entity vanished after a
    Z-Wave exclusion) no update listener is registered, so an options-flow
    save just sits in ``options``. The data→options migration merges
    options-preferred, so a reauth that only wrote into ``data`` would lose
    to the stale options on the next load. The reauth write must therefore
    consume the pending options (options-preferred, its own input winning)
    and clear them.
    """
    entry = lock_code_manager_config_entry
    entry.async_start_reauth(hass, context={"lock_entity_id": LOCK_1_ENTITY_ID})
    await hass.async_block_till_done()

    # An options-flow save the failed entry could never process.
    stale_options = {**BASE_CONFIG, CONF_LOCKS: [LOCK_2_ENTITY_ID]}
    hass.config_entries.async_update_entry(entry, options=stale_options)

    [flow] = entry.async_get_active_flows(hass, {SOURCE_REAUTH})
    result = await hass.config_entries.flow.async_configure(
        flow["flow_id"], {CONF_LOCKS: [LOCK_1_ENTITY_ID, LOCK_2_ENTITY_ID]}
    )
    assert result["reason"] == "locks_updated"
    await hass.async_block_till_done()

    # The reauth's lock list won and the stale options were consumed, so the
    # next data→options migration has nothing stale to prefer.
    assert entry.data[CONF_LOCKS] == [LOCK_1_ENTITY_ID, LOCK_2_ENTITY_ID]
    assert not entry.options

def _user_code_caps(num_slots: int) -> dict:
    """
    Return a User Code CC capabilities response with the given slot count.

    ``num_slots=0`` is the degenerate interview-incomplete shape from issue
    #1298; a positive count is the recovered/healthy shape. Everything else
    matches what the driver reports for a User Code CC dispatch (no user
    management, spec-default 4-10 code length).
    """
    pin_type_str = lock_helpers.CREDENTIAL_TYPE_MAP[UserCredentialType.PIN_CODE]
    return {
        "supports_user_management": False,
        "max_users": 0,
        "supported_user_types": [],
        "max_user_name_length": 0,
        "supported_credential_rules": [],
        "supported_credential_types": {
            pin_type_str: {
                "num_slots": num_slots,
                "min_length": 4,
                "max_length": 10,
                "supports_learn": False,
            }
        },
    }

input.command:
git add custom_components tests && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: suspend sync for unvalidated locks; make reauth writes win over stale options

Follow-ups from the post-#1344/#1345 review:

- sync: a lock whose provider setup has not validated (deferred or
  structurally failed) now suspends its sync managers instead of
  attempting writes. Writes against such a lock could only fail on the
  capability probe, landing in the generic error handler with a
  misleading "report this bug" suspension repair (or, on matter,
  disabling the slot) stacked on the accurate lock_setup_failed repair
  -- and, on Z-Wave, re-firing the slot-count recovery device query on
  every attempt. Reconciliation resumes via request_sync_check once the
  LOADED-transition revalidation succeeds. New public
  BaseLock.provider_setup_succeeded property backs the gate.

- providers: reset _setup_succeeded when revalidation fails on the
  reconnect path, so a previously-healthy lock that degrades (e.g. a
  re-interview zeroed its slot count) re-gates sync instead of keeping
  stale success state.

- config_flow: the reauth write now consumes pending options
  (options-preferred, its own input winning) and clears them. An
  options-flow save made while the entry was failed would otherwise
  override the newer reauth fix on the next load, because the
  data->options migration merges options-preferred.

- zwave_js: guard the recovery's root-endpoint lookup with .get(0) so a
  KeyError cannot escape past the typed handlers and drop the lock.

- tests: collapse the duplicated zero-slot/healthy capability dict
  builders into one _user_code_caps(num_slots) factory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1 && git push -u origin fix/degraded-lock-sync-gating 2>&1 | tail -1

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: suspend sync for unvalidated locks; make reauth writes win over stale options" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Proposed change

Follow-ups from the review of #1344/#1345:

1. **Sync managers now suspend while a lock's provider setup has not validated** (deferred or structurally failed), resuming via `request_sync_check` once the LOADED-transition revalidation succeeds. Without this gate, every tick against a degraded lock attempted a write that could only fail on the capability probe — landing in the generic error handler with a misleading "may indicate a bug … report this issue" suspension repair (or, on matter, a `CodeRejectedError` → slot disabled + active switch off) stacked on top of the accurate `lock_setup_failed` repair, and re-firing the Z-Wave slot-count recovery device query (radio traffic against a battery lock) on every attempt. This also makes the `lock_setup_failed` repair text in #1345 ("code synchronization is paused for this lock") true. Backed by a new public `BaseLock.provider_setup_succeeded` property.

2. **`_setup_succeeded` resets when revalidation fails on the reconnect path**, so a previously-healthy lock that degrades (e.g. a re-interview zeroed its slot count) re-gates sync instead of keeping stale success state.

3. **Reauth writes now consume pending options and clear them** (options-preferred merge with the reauth input winning). An options-flow save made while the entry was failed sits unprocessed in `options`; since the data→options migration merges options-preferred, it would silently override the newer reauth fix on the next load.

4. **The Z-Wave recovery's root-endpoint lookup uses `.get(0)`** so a `KeyError` cannot escape past the typed handlers and drop the lock.

5. Tests: the duplicated zero-slot/healthy capability dict builders collapse into one `_user_code_caps(num_slots)` factory.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [x] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [ ] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information

- This PR is related to issue: follow-up to #1344 and #1345

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

85	def _check_common_slots(
86	    hass: HomeAssistant,
87	    locks: Iterable[str],
88	    slots_list: Iterable[int | str],
89	    config_entry: ConfigEntry | None = None,
90	) -> tuple[dict, dict]:
91	    """Check if slots are already configured."""
92	    try:
93	        lock, common_slots, entry_title = next(
94	            (lock, common_slots, entry.title)
95	            for lock in locks
96	            for entry in hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
97	            if (config := get_entry_config(entry)).has_lock(lock)
98	            and (
99	                common_slots := sorted(set(config.slots) & {int(s) for s in slots_list})
100	            )
101	            and not (config_entry and config_entry == entry)
102	        )
103	    except StopIteration:
104	        return {}, {}
105	    else:
106	        return {"base": "slots_already_configured"}, {
107	            "common_slots": ", ".join(str(slot) for slot in common_slots),
108	            "lock": lock,
109	            "entry_title": entry_title,
110	        }

118	) -> tuple[dict | None, dict, dict]:
119	    """
120	    Validate a slots-YAML submission for the config and options flows.
121	
122	    Runs ``CODE_SLOTS_SCHEMA`` over ``raw_slots`` and, when it parses cleanly,
123	    checks for slots already configured on the same locks by another entry.
124	    Returns the parsed slots (or ``None`` if validation failed) along with the
125	    accumulated error and description-placeholder dicts.
126	    """
127	    try:
128	        parsed_slots = CODE_SLOTS_SCHEMA(raw_slots)
129	    except vol.Invalid as err:
130	        _LOGGER.error("Invalid YAML: %s", err)
131	        return None, {"base": "invalid_config"}, {}
132	
133	    errors, placeholders = _check_common_slots(hass, locks, parsed_slots, config_entry)
134	    return parsed_slots, errors, placeholders

141	def _async_build_lock_instance(
142	    hass: HomeAssistant,
143	    dev_reg: dr.DeviceRegistry,
144	    ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
145	    lock_entity_id: str,
146	) -> Any:
147	    """
148	    Build a temporary lock provider instance for ``lock_entity_id``.
149	
150	    Performs setup-time checks (entity in registry, supported platform,
151	    parent config entry exists) and instantiates the provider class.
152	    Raises ``_LockQuerySkipped`` if any setup-time check fails.
153	    """
154	    lock_entry = ent_reg.async_get(lock_entity_id)
155	    if not lock_entry:
156	        _LOGGER.warning(
157	            "Entity %s not found in registry; skipping usercode check",
158	            lock_entity_id,
159	        )
160	        raise _LockQuerySkipped(lock_entity_id)
161	    if lock_entry.platform not in INTEGRATIONS_CLASS_MAP:
162	        _LOGGER.debug(
163	            "Lock %s uses unsupported platform %s; skipping usercode check",
164	            lock_entity_id,
165	            lock_entry.platform,
166	        )
167	        raise _LockQuerySkipped(lock_entity_id)
168	    lock_config_entry = hass.config_entries.async_get_entry(lock_entry.config_entry_id)
169	    if lock_config_entry is None:
170	        _LOGGER.warning(
171	            "Config entry for lock %s not found; skipping usercode check",
172	            lock_entity_id,
173	        )
174	        raise _LockQuerySkipped(lock_entity_id)
175	
176	    return INTEGRATIONS_CLASS_MAP[lock_entry.platform](
177	        hass, dev_reg, ent_reg, lock_config_entry, lock_entry
178	    )

181	async def _async_get_all_codes(
182	    hass: HomeAssistant,
183	    dev_reg: dr.DeviceRegistry,
184	    ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
185	    lock_entity_ids: list[str],
186	) -> dict[str, dict[int, SlotCredential]]:
187	    """
188	    Query locks for all usercodes.
189	
190	    Returns ``codes_by_lock`` mapping each lock entity ID to its slot/credential
191	    dict (``SlotCredential.empty()`` for empty slots).  Locks that fail to query
192	    are skipped with logging.
193	    """
194	    result: dict[str, dict[int, SlotCredential]] = {}
195	    # Query sequentially to avoid flooding networks (e.g. Z-Wave, Matter)
196	    # with simultaneous requests across multiple locks
197	    for lock_entity_id in lock_entity_ids:
198	        try:
199	            lock_instance = _async_build_lock_instance(
200	                hass, dev_reg, ent_reg, lock_entity_id
201	            )
202	            usercodes = await lock_instance.async_internal_get_usercodes()
203	        except _LockQuerySkipped:
204	            continue
205	        except LockCodeManagerError as err:
206	            _LOGGER.warning(
207	                "Failed to get usercodes from %s: %s",
208	                lock_entity_id,
209	                err,
210	            )
211	            continue
212	        except Exception:
213	            _LOGGER.warning(
214	                "Failed to get usercodes from %s; this lock's codes will not be shown",
215	                lock_entity_id,
216	                exc_info=True,
217	            )
218	            continue
219	
220	        if usercodes:
221	            result[lock_entity_id] = usercodes
222	    return result

"""Utility functions for lock_code_manager."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Iterable
import logging
import re
from typing import Any
import zlib

from homeassistant.components.switch import DOMAIN as SWITCH_DOMAIN, SERVICE_TURN_OFF
from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry
from homeassistant.const import ATTR_ENTITY_ID, CONF_ENABLED, CONF_PIN
from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant
from homeassistant.helpers import entity_registry as er
from homeassistant.helpers.issue_registry import IssueSeverity, async_create_issue

from ..const import DOMAIN
from .config import EntryConfig, build_slot_unique_id

_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def mask_pin(
    pin: str | None,
    slot_num: int | str,
    instance_id: str,
) -> str:
    """
    Return a deterministic masked representation of a PIN for logging.

    Uses CRC32 salted with the HA instance ID and slot number so the same
    PIN on the same slot always produces the same 8-char hex token regardless
    of which lock or config entry it belongs to.  Different slots or HA
    instances produce different tokens.
    """
    if not pin:
        return "<empty>"
    salt = f"{instance_id}:{slot_num}:{pin}"
    return f"pin#{zlib.crc32(salt.encode()) & 0xFFFFFFFF:08x}"


# Token format emitted by ``mask_pin``: literal "pin#" followed by 8 lowercase
# hex chars. Anchored by word boundary on the right so a longer hex string
# isn't truncated and reported as a match.
_PIN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"pin#[0-9a-f]{8}\b")


def build_pin_deobfuscation_map(
    entries: Iterable[ConfigEntry], instance_id: str
) -> dict[str, str]:
    """
    Build a ``{masked_token: plaintext_pin}`` lookup for every configured PIN.

    Reads each entry's options-or-data view via ``EntryConfig`` so it works
    whether or not the entry has been migrated to options yet. Slots whose
    PIN is empty are skipped because ``mask_pin`` returns ``<empty>`` for
    them and that's not a token we need to reverse.

    Slot is part of the salt, so different ``(slot, pin)`` pairs produce
    different tokens with overwhelming probability — CRC32 has a 32-bit
    output so collisions are mathematically possible but vanishingly
    unlikely at any plausible slot count. The resulting map has one
    entry per configured slot with a PIN.
    """
    table: dict[str, str] = {}
    for entry in entries:
        config = EntryConfig.from_entry(entry)
        for slot_num, slot_config in config.slots.items():
            pin = slot_config.get(CONF_PIN)
            if not pin:
                continue
            table[mask_pin(pin, slot_num, instance_id)] = pin
    return table


def deobfuscate_pins(text: str, table: dict[str, str]) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
    """
    Replace ``pin#xxxxxxxx`` tokens in ``text`` using ``table``.

    Tokens not in the table are left verbatim so the output stays
    paste-compatible with the original log (useful when only some PINs
    have been rotated since the log was written). The summary lists the
    distinct unmatched tokens so the caller can see what didn't resolve
    without scanning the text.
    """
    counts = {"total": 0, "matched": 0}
    unmatched: set[str] = set()

    def _replace(match: re.Match[str]) -> str:
        token = match.group(0)
        counts["total"] += 1
        if token in table:
            counts["matched"] += 1
            return table[token]
        unmatched.add(token)
        return token

    deobfuscated = _PIN_TOKEN_RE.sub(_replace, text)
    summary: dict[str, Any] = {
        "total": counts["total"],
        "matched": counts["matched"],
        "unmatched_tokens": sorted(unmatched),
    }
    return deobfuscated, summary


async def async_disable_slot(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
    ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
    config_entry_id: str,
    slot_num: int,
    *,
    reason: str | None = None,
    lock_name: str | None = None,
    lock_entity_id: str | None = None,
) -> bool:
    """
    Disable a slot via the enabled switch and optionally create a repair issue.

    Returns True if the switch was found and turned off, False otherwise.
    When reason is provided, a repair issue is created so the user can
    acknowledge it through the Home Assistant repairs dashboard.
    """
    enabled_entity_id = ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
        SWITCH_DOMAIN,
        DOMAIN,
        build_slot_unique_id(config_entry_id, slot_num, CONF_ENABLED),
    )
    if not enabled_entity_id:
        lock_context = f" on {lock_name} ({lock_entity_id})" if lock_name else ""
        _LOGGER.warning(
            "Cannot disable slot %s%s — switch entity not found",
            slot_num,
            lock_context,
        )
        return False

    await hass.services.async_call(
        SWITCH_DOMAIN,
        SERVICE_TURN_OFF,
        {ATTR_ENTITY_ID: enabled_entity_id},
        blocking=True,
    )

    if reason:
        issue_id = f"slot_disabled_{config_entry_id}_{slot_num}"
        async_create_issue(
            hass,
            DOMAIN,
            issue_id,
            is_fixable=True,
            is_persistent=True,
            severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
            translation_key="slot_disabled",
            translation_placeholders={
                "slot_num": str(slot_num),
                "reason": reason,
            },
        )

    return True

.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_init.py tests/providers/test_base.py tests/test_coordinator.py -q 2>&1 | tail -1 && git add custom_components && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor: build per-lock repair issue ids through one helper

The lock_setup_failed and lock_offline issue ids were each hand-built as
f-strings at three independent sites (create, clear, entry-removal
cleanup) across three files, and async_remove_entry re-enumerated every
per-lock issue key inline -- a new per-lock repair type would orphan its
persistent issue on entry removal unless a developer remembered to add
another delete line. per_lock_issue_id() now owns the id shape and
async_remove_entry iterates PER_LOCK_ISSUE_KEYS.

Also clear the setup-failed repair inline where setup success is
established instead of re-deriving success from the flag in a trailing
else clause (review follow-up on this branch).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1 && git push 2>&1 | tail -1

content.text:
All review findings are now fixed across the two open PRs, and the merge you asked for is done. Final state:

**PR [#1345](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/pull/1345)** (lock_setup_failed repair — open, now MERGEABLE against main):
- Merged main in (resolved the expected squash-overlap conflicts in `_base.py`/`test_base.py` by keeping the stacked changes), so its diff is a clean 6 files again.
- Pushed the F4 consolidation: `per_lock_issue_id()` + `PER_LOCK_ISSUE_KEYS` in `domain/util.py` now own the issue-id shape; `_base.py`, `coordinator.py` (lock_offline), and `async_remove_entry` all build ids through it, and entry-removal cleanup iterates the key tuple so a future per-lock repair type can't orphan.
- Pushed F6: the repair clear now sits inline right after `_setup_succeeded = True` instead of the flag-re-deriving `else` clause.

**PR [#1346](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/pull/1346)** (new branch off main, per your instruction since #1344 is merged):
- **Sync gate (the confirmed top finding):** sync managers suspend while `provider_setup_succeeded` is False and resume via `request_sync_check` after revalidation — no more misleading "report this bug" suspensions / matter slot-disables against degraded locks, no more per-write recovery re-fires, and #1345's "synchronization is paused" repair text becomes true.
- **Reauth precedence:** the reauth write consumes pending options (its input winning) and clears them, so a stale options save can no longer silently revert a reauth fix.
- Plus the flag reset on failed revalidation (now load-bearing since it gates sync), the `endpoints.get(0)` guard, and the `_user_code_caps(num_slots)` test factory.

Both branches: full suite green (1269 passed) and prek clean. One heads-up: #1345 and #1346 touch adjacent regions of `async_setup_internal`, so whichever merges second may need a trivial conflict resolution — merge #1345 first and I can merge main forward into #1346 after, if you like.

Also from this checkpoint cycle: mempalace was unreachable (all calls erroring), so the session state went into file-based memory (`project_degraded_lock_followups.md`) instead — worth a look at the mempalace server when you get a chance.

1	"""Utility functions for lock_code_manager."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from collections.abc import Iterable
6	import logging
7	import re
8	from typing import Any
9	import zlib
10	
11	from homeassistant.components.switch import DOMAIN as SWITCH_DOMAIN, SERVICE_TURN_OFF
12	from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry
13	from homeassistant.const import ATTR_ENTITY_ID, CONF_ENABLED, CONF_PIN
14	from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant
15	from homeassistant.helpers import entity_registry as er
16	from homeassistant.helpers.issue_registry import IssueSeverity, async_create_issue
17	
18	from ..const import DOMAIN
19	from .config import EntryConfig, build_slot_unique_id
20	
21	_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	def mask_pin(
25	    pin: str | None,
26	    slot_num: int | str,
27	    instance_id: str,
28	) -> str:
29	    """
30	    Return a deterministic masked representation of a PIN for logging.
31	
32	    Uses CRC32 salted with the HA instance ID and slot number so the same
33	    PIN on the same slot always produces the same 8-char hex token regardless
34	    of which lock or config entry it belongs to.  Different slots or HA
35	    instances produce different tokens.
36	    """
37	    if not pin:
38	        return "<empty>"
39	    salt = f"{instance_id}:{slot_num}:{pin}"
40	    return f"pin#{zlib.crc32(salt.encode()) & 0xFFFFFFFF:08x}"
41	
42	
43	# Token format emitted by ``mask_pin``: literal "pin#" followed by 8 lowercase
44	# hex chars. Anchored by word boundary on the right so a longer hex string
45	# isn't truncated and reported as a match.
46	_PIN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"pin#[0-9a-f]{8}\b")
47	
48	
49	def build_pin_deobfuscation_map(
50	    entries: Iterable[ConfigEntry], instance_id: str
51	) -> dict[str, str]:
52	    """
53	    Build a ``{masked_token: plaintext_pin}`` lookup for every configured PIN.
54	
55	    Reads each entry's options-or-data view via ``EntryConfig`` so it works
56	    whether or not the entry has been migrated to options yet. Slots whose
57	    PIN is empty are skipped because ``mask_pin`` returns ``<empty>`` for
58	    them and that's not a token we need to reverse.
59	
60	    Slot is part of the salt, so different ``(slot, pin)`` pairs produce
61	    different tokens with overwhelming probability — CRC32 has a 32-bit
62	    output so collisions are mathematically possible but vanishingly
63	    unlikely at any plausible slot count. The resulting map has one
64	    entry per configured slot with a PIN.
65	    """
66	    table: dict[str, str] = {}
67	    for entry in entries:
68	        config = EntryConfig.from_entry(entry)
69	        for slot_num, slot_config in config.slots.items():
70	            pin = slot_config.get(CONF_PIN)
71	            if not pin:
72	                continue
73	            table[mask_pin(pin, slot_num, instance_id)] = pin
74	    return table
75	
76	
77	def deobfuscate_pins(text: str, table: dict[str, str]) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
78	    """
79	    Replace ``pin#xxxxxxxx`` tokens in ``text`` using ``table``.
80	
81	    Tokens not in the table are left verbatim so the output stays
82	    paste-compatible with the original log (useful when only some PINs
83	    have been rotated since the log was written). The summary lists the
84	    distinct unmatched tokens so the caller can see what didn't resolve
85	    without scanning the text.
86	    """
87	    counts = {"total": 0, "matched": 0}
88	    unmatched: set[str] = set()
89	
90	    def _replace(match: re.Match[str]) -> str:
91	        token = match.group(0)
92	        counts["total"] += 1
93	        if token in table:
94	            counts["matched"] += 1
95	            return table[token]
96	        unmatched.add(token)
97	        return token
98	
99	    deobfuscated = _PIN_TOKEN_RE.sub(_replace, text)
100	    summary: dict[str, Any] = {
101	        "total": counts["total"],
102	        "matched": counts["matched"],
103	        "unmatched_tokens": sorted(unmatched),
104	    }
105	    return deobfuscated, summary
106	
107	
108	async def async_disable_slot(
109	    hass: HomeAssistant,
110	    ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
111	    config_entry_id: str,
112	    slot_num: int,
113	    *,
114	    reason: str | None = None,
115	    lock_name: str | None = None,
116	    lock_entity_id: str | None = None,
117	) -> bool:
118	    """
119	    Disable a slot via the enabled switch and optionally create a repair issue.
120	
121	    Returns True if the switch was found and turned off, False otherwise.
122	    When reason is provided, a repair issue is created so the user can
123	    acknowledge it through the Home Assistant repairs dashboard.
124	    """
125	    enabled_entity_id = ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
126	        SWITCH_DOMAIN,
127	        DOMAIN,
128	        build_slot_unique_id(config_entry_id, slot_num, CONF_ENABLED),
129	    )
130	    if not enabled_entity_id:
131	        lock_context = f" on {lock_name} ({lock_entity_id})" if lock_name else ""
132	        _LOGGER.warning(
133	            "Cannot disable slot %s%s — switch entity not found",
134	            slot_num,
135	            lock_context,
136	        )
137	        return False
138	
139	    await hass.services.async_call(
140	        SWITCH_DOMAIN,
141	        SERVICE_TURN_OFF,
142	        {ATTR_ENTITY_ID: enabled_entity_id},
143	        blocking=True,
144	    )
145	
146	    if reason:
147	        issue_id = f"slot_disabled_{config_entry_id}_{slot_num}"
148	        async_create_issue(
149	            hass,
150	            DOMAIN,
151	            issue_id,
152	            is_fixable=True,
153	            is_persistent=True,
154	            severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
155	            translation_key="slot_disabled",
156	            translation_placeholders={
157	                "slot_num": str(slot_num),
158	                "reason": reason,
159	            },
160	        )
161	
162	    return True

113	async def async_migrate_entry(
114	    hass: HomeAssistant, config_entry: LockCodeManagerConfigEntry
115	) -> bool:
116	    """Migrate old entry data to new format."""
117	    if config_entry.version == 1:
118	        _LOGGER.debug(
119	            "%s (%s): Migrating from version 1 to 2",
120	            config_entry.entry_id,
121	            config_entry.title,
122	        )
123	
124	        # Migrate CONF_CALENDAR to CONF_ENTITY_ID in slot configs
125	        new_data = {**config_entry.data}
126	        new_options = {**config_entry.options}
127	
128	        for data_dict in (new_data, new_options):
129	            if CONF_SLOTS in data_dict:
130	                new_slots = {}
131	                for slot_num, slot_config in data_dict[CONF_SLOTS].items():
132	                    new_slot = {**slot_config}
133	                    # Migrate calendar to entity_id if not already set
134	                    if CONF_CALENDAR in new_slot and CONF_ENTITY_ID not in new_slot:
135	                        new_slot[CONF_ENTITY_ID] = new_slot.pop(CONF_CALENDAR)
136	                    elif CONF_CALENDAR in new_slot:
137	                        # Remove calendar if entity_id is already set
138	                        new_slot.pop(CONF_CALENDAR)
139	                    new_slots[slot_num] = new_slot
140	                data_dict[CONF_SLOTS] = new_slots
141	
142	        hass.config_entries.async_update_entry(
143	            config_entry, data=new_data, options=new_options, version=2
144	        )
145	        _LOGGER.info(
146	            "%s (%s): Migration to version 2 complete",
147	            config_entry.entry_id,
148	            config_entry.title,
149	        )
150	
151	    if config_entry.version == 2:
152	        # Strip the deprecated number_of_uses field from slot configs and
153	        # surface a one-time informational repair pointing users to the Slot
154	        # Usage Limiter blueprint replacement. Running here (before setup)
155	        # ensures the deprecated field never reaches any EntryConfig consumer.
156	        async_delete_issue(hass, DOMAIN, "number_of_uses_deprecated")
157	        new_data = {**config_entry.data}
158	        new_options = {**config_entry.options}
159	        entry_impacted: set[str] = set()
160	        for data_dict in (new_data, new_options):
161	            if CONF_SLOTS not in data_dict:
162	                continue
163	            new_slots = {}
164	            for slot_num, slot_config in data_dict[CONF_SLOTS].items():
165	                new_slot = {**slot_config}
166	                if "number_of_uses" in new_slot:
167	                    new_slot.pop("number_of_uses")
168	                    entry_impacted.add(str(slot_num))
169	                new_slots[slot_num] = new_slot
170	            data_dict[CONF_SLOTS] = new_slots
171	        hass.config_entries.async_update_entry(

content.text:
Done. Main (now containing #1346) is merged into `fix/lock-setup-failed-repair` and pushed — PR **#1345** is MERGEABLE against main again.

The one conflict was the semantic overlap I flagged earlier: both PRs added a line to the same `except LockCodeManagerProviderError` branch in `_async_on_integration_loaded`. I resolved it by keeping both — the `_setup_succeeded = False` reset from #1346 (so sync stays gated on revalidation failure) followed by this branch's `_report_setup_validation_failure(err)` (so the repair issue is raised). That combination is exactly the intended end state: a lock that degrades after being healthy both re-gates sync and surfaces the repair.

Full suite passes on the merged result — 1272 passed (the 3 extra tests being #1346's sync-gate and reauth regressions now running alongside this branch's repair tests). #1345 is ready to merge whenever you are.

101	        return "state", {

116	    Base class for lock provider implementations.

118	    Data Fetching Modes

120	    The coordinator supports three update modes. All modes include an initial poll.

122	    1. Poll for updates (default):

124	       - Used when supports_push = False

126	
127	    2. Push for updates:

129	       - Enabled when supports_push = True

133	    3. Poll for drift:

135	       - Detects out-of-band changes (e.g., codes changed at keypad)

137	       - Set hard_refresh_interval = None to disable

141	       - Helps detect reconnects for integrations without config entry state signals

143
